export_view.value:
Custom Forms are documents completed on tickets to ensure the Sales and Repair process is thorough and complete. There are many customizable fields to assign to a form, based on ticket types and actions. Users can create triggers and requirements for forms to be completed by your staff or a customer before the ticket can be completed. Custom Forms also works alongside Qpop Companion to create a customer-facing screen to view and sign digital forms.

A few examples of forms that can be created are:

  • Repair Intake Forms

  • Repair Waivers/Work Authorizations

  • Trade-In Condition Checklists

  • Pre/Post Repair Checklists

  • Device Condition Image Uploads

Admin users can create forms for a specific location or globally (all locations), while Location Admins can create and edit forms for any location they are assigned.

Note: Custom Form creation is available to users on our Essential plan and higher. Starter Plan users can use default forms to use on tickets.

Additional Resources

  • Introduction to Custom Forms - Start Here!

  • Working with Custom Forms on Tickets

  • Custom Forms: Field Groups and Form Fields

  • Custom Forms: Adding Images to Forms

  • Custom Forms: Capturing Electronic Signatures on Forms

Creating Custom Form Field Groups

Field Groups are the highest level of your custom forms. These include user-defined requirements that change the behavior of the form. A Form Field Group will define which ticket types will have forms and when those forms need to be filled out by either the technician or the customer.

On the Navigation Bar, go to Settings → Location Settings. On the Settings Overview page, the left column has additional settings categories. Click Tickets to expand the dropdown and then select Custom Forms.

On the Custom Forms page, click Add Custom Form. Begin filling out the Custom Field Groups. These are user-defined requirements and triggers that change the behavior of the form. Required fields are marked with an Asterisk (*). When you are satisfied with your selections, click Save field group settings.

Note: For an in-depth explanation of all Field Groups, check out Custom Forms: Field Groups and Form Fields.

  • Title - Assign a title to identify this form.

  • Location ID - Select which location(s) will have access to this form.

  • Ticket Type - Select which ticket type(s) will include this form to fill out during the flow.

  • Required When - If this form will be required to be filled out, select the timing of when the form will appear based on a ticket’s status change.

  • Active - Select whether this form will be active on tickets or inactive.

Adding Form Fields to your Custom Form

Once your form is created, Form Fields are used to populate the form with data collection fields. These fields can be customized to add supporting details, answer binary questions, make selections, and even upload files/photos. Required fields are marked with an Asterisk (*).

After creating the form Field Group, there will be a section at the bottom of the page to add Form Fields. Click on the dropdown to select the field you want to add.

Note: For an in-depth explanation of Form Fields, check out Custom Forms: Field Groups and Form Fields.

Begin filling out the Form Field. Required fields are marked with an Asterisk (*). In this scenario, we are adding a text field to enter the Serial/IMEI of a device. At a minimum, it is helpful to add a label and a placeholder. When you are satisfied with your changes, click Save Changes.

Note: If you would like to include this information when printing a copy of your Custom Form, make sure Print Visible? is checked.

If you would like to add more fields, you can click the Add a field dropdown to select another field. In this case, we will add a Checkbox field.

Begin filling out the Form Field. Required fields are marked with an Asterisk (*). In this scenario, we add a checkbox field to mark whether or not the device has water damage. When you are satisfied with your changes, click Save Changes.

Note: By default, the checkbox is unselected. If you would like to change the checkbox default to marked, type Yes in the Default Value field.

Dynamic Form Fields

Text Input, Text Area, and HTML Form Fields now have a Dynamic Value dropdown, allowing users to insert shortcodes into the text area, allowing for more customized forms. Similar to Automated Notifications, Shortcodes will be replaced with the appropriate data depending on the context of the form.

Editing and Sorting Form Fields

If you need to make changes or delete a Form Field, available options are under the Actions column of the Form Fields list. You can change the order of your fields by clicking and dragging the icon under the Order column on the far left.

Cloning Custom Forms

If you need to create similar forms, you can save time by cloning an existing form. When viewing the Custom Form list, click the Copy button (duplicate page icon) to create a duplicate form that will carry over all form details and fields.

The new form will be identified as a copy of the original form on the list.

You can also clone Form Fields by clicking the Copy button next to an existing field.

START 1.21.X
